DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT CIVIC CENTER INN, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS OF PLAINTIFF JACKSON CEBULLA TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET NO. 1, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET NO. 1, AND DEMAND FOR PRODUCTIONAND INSPECTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET NO. 1; REQUEST FOR ORDER AWARDING MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF. THE UNOPPOSED MOTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF JACKSON CEBULLA SHALL PROVIDE RESPONSIVE ANSWERS TO THE FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, AND DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION AND INSPECTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, BY APRIL 30, 2024. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED TO DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,162.50. THESE SANCTIONS COVER BOTH LINES 1 AND 2 SINCE THE WORK IS DUPLICATIVE EXCEPT FOR THE NAME OF THE RESPONDING PARTY. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO JUDGE PRO TEM TOM COHEN AT TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM. Motion To Compel Answers Of Plaintiff Jackson Cebulla To Form Interrogatories, Set No. 1, Special Interrogatories, Set No. 1, And Demand For Productionand Inspection Of Documents, Set No. 1; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff
Off Calendar Apr-19-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Ilan N. Rosen Janfaza, Esq., The Law Offices of Ilan N. Rosen Janfaza (310) 550-6000 : litigation@hotelinjurylaw.com Attorneys for Plaintiffs, JACKSON CEBULLA and PATRICIA FALCON-CEBULLA
No other appearances are made.

Plaintiff's counsel does not stipualte Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen deciding today's motion.

Court advises plaintiff that no opposition was filed in response to todays' motions. Since the motion is unopposed, Plaintiff does not have any standing to contest this motion and is deemed to stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem.

Not ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CIVIC CENTER INN, LLC Motion To Compel Answers Of Plaintiff Jackson Cebulla To Form Interrogatories, Set No. 1, Special Interrogatories, Set No. 1, And Demand For Productionand Inspection Of Documents, Set No. 1; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff

The unopposed motion is granted. Plaintiff JACKSON CEBULLA shall provide responsive answers to the FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE and SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, and DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION AND INSPECTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, by April 30, 2024.

Sanctions are awarded to Defendant in the amount of $1,162.50. These sanctions cover both lines 1 and 2 since the work is duplicative except for the name of the responding party.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)

The unopposed motion is granted. The truth of the matters specified in Defendant's Requests For Admissions, Set No. 1 are deemed admitted.

Sanctions are awarded to Defendant in the amount of $672.50. These sanctions cover both lines 3 and 4 since the work is duplicative except for the name of the responding party.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com.
